text.title: Meta Ads Campaign Setup Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-meta-campaign-setup methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Campaign objective MUST match the actual conversion event (Conversions/Leads for purchase/lead; Reach/Awareness only when no conversion exists).
rule.rationale: Wrong objective optimizes on the wrong signal; Meta delivers cheap impressions but no conversions.

rule.statement: Maximum 5 ad sets per campaign. Above that, delivery fragments and no ad set reaches the 50-conversion/week learning threshold.
rule.rationale: Below 50 conversions per ad set per week the campaign is stuck in learning forever.

rule.statement: Use CBO (Advantage Campaign Budget) only when total conversions ≥50/week. Below that, use ABO with explicit ad-set budgets.
rule.rationale: CBO under-rewards low-volume ad sets and starves smaller audiences; ABO gives manual budget control.

rule.statement: Default to Advantage+ Placements unless we have ≥30 days of placement-level CPM/CPA showing a specific placement is loss-making.
rule.rationale: Manual placement selection limits delivery and historically increases CPM 20-40% without conversion lift.

rule.statement: Naming MUST follow the [stage]_[product]_[audience]_[creative]_[date] convention. No personal initials, no ad-hoc names.
rule.rationale: Reporting across ad accounts breaks without a deterministic naming convention.
